perturbation.original_text: During the Term of this Agreement, Premier shall be required to purchase a Minimum Annual Order Volume ("MAOV") of [***] ("Units") for the twelve-month period commencing July 1, 2018 and ending June 30, 2019. The MAOV [***] Units for each twelve-month period commencing July 1, 2019 through the end of the Term, contingent upon commercial aseptic production at Heritage's [***] facility and approval of that facility by Premier by January 1, 2019.
perturbation.changed_text: During the Term of this Agreement, Premier may consider purchasing a Suggested Yearly Order Quantity ("SYOQ") of [***] ("Units") for the twelve-month period commencing July 1, 2018 and ending June 30, 2019. The SYOQ [***] Units for each twelve-month period commencing July 1, 2019 through the end of the Term, depending on commercial aseptic production at Heritage's [***] facility and approval of that facility by Premier by January 1, 2019.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Minimum Annual Order Volume' ('MAOV') is replaced with 'Suggested Yearly Order Quantity' ('SYOQ'). Additionally, 'shall be required to purchase' is changed to 'may consider purchasing' and 'contingent upon' to 'depending on', implying that the purchase is no longer mandatory. This undermines the binding nature of the agreement established earlier.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties hereby agree to remove Section 2(c) in its entirety and replace it with the following: During the Term of this Agreement, Premier may consider purchasing a Suggested Yearly Order Quantity ("SYOQ") of [***] ("Units") for the twelve-month period commencing July 1, 2018 and ending June 30, 2019. The SYOQ [***] Units for each twelve-month period commencing July 1, 2019 through the end of the Term, depending on commercial aseptic production at Heritage's [***] facility and approval of that facility by Premier by January 1, 2019. For the avoidance of doubt, the six-month period commencing July 1, 2022 and ending December 31, 2022, Premier will be required to purchase [***] (the twelve-month periods and the six-month period are each a "Contract Period").
perturbation.justification: The original text mandated a 'Minimum Annual Order Volume' (MAOV), creating a binding obligation. The changed text replaces this with a 'Suggested Yearly Order Quantity' (SYOQ) and uses permissive language ('may consider purchasing', 'depending on'), effectively removing the mandatory aspect. This directly contradicts the 'For the avoidance of doubt, the six-month period commencing July 1, 2022 and ending December 31, 2022, Premier will be required to purchase [***]' statement in the contradicted text, which still implies a required purchase within the Term, hence creating an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: For the avoidance of doubt, the six-month period commencing July 1, 2022 and ending December 31, 2022, Premier will be required to purchase [***] (the twelve-month periods and the six-month period are each a "Contract Period").
perturbation.changed_text: For the avoidance of doubt, the six-month period commencing July 1, 2022 and ending December 31, 2022, Premier should attempt to procure [***] (the twelve-month periods and the six-month period are each a "Contract Milestone").
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Contract Period' is replaced with 'Contract Milestone,' creating ambiguity about whether these periods are deadlines or simply markers of progress. Also, 'will be required to purchase' is changed to 'should attempt to procure,' further weakening the purchase obligation. This is a contradiction, because it is not clear if it is still mandatory to purchase, creating confusion within the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties hereby agree to remove Section 2(c) in its entirety and replace it with the following: During the Term of this Agreement, Premier may consider purchasing a Suggested Yearly Order Quantity ("SYOQ") of [***] ("Units") for the twelve-month period commencing July 1, 2018 and ending June 30, 2019. The SYOQ [***] Units for each twelve-month period commencing July 1, 2019 through the end of the Term, depending on commercial aseptic production at Heritage's [***] facility and approval of that facility by Premier by January 1, 2019. For the avoidance of doubt, the six-month period commencing July 1, 2022 and ending December 31, 2022, Premier should attempt to procure [***] (the twelve-month periods and the six-month period are each a "Contract Milestone").
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Contract Period' with 'Contract Milestone' and 'will be required to purchase' with 'should attempt to procure'. This creates a contradiction because the contradicted text uses the changed text and now has a clause that creates ambiguity. The contradicted text now contains language that uses the term 'Contract Milestone' instead of 'Contract Period' and also replaces the mandatory purchase obligation with 'should attempt to procure'. This directly impacts the enforceability of the purchase obligations, creating a clear contradiction and a potential legal issue because it is unclear whether Premier is now required to purchase, which is a key point of the agreement. This adheres to the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition because it replaces the terms 'Contract Period' to 'Contract Milestone' without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations because a 'Contract Period' implies a timeframe with mandatory purchase obligations, whereas 'Contract Milestone' implies a progress marker, and not necessarily a hard deadline.

perturbation.original_text: Except as otherwise specified above in this Amendment, all other terms, conditions and covenants of the Agreement shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.changed_text: Except as otherwise suggested above in this Amendment, all other terms, understandings and recommendations of the Agreement shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing 'specified', 'conditions', 'covenants' with 'suggested', 'understandings', and 'recommendations' respectively, it undermines the legally binding nature of the original terms, conditions, and covenants. This modification makes the entire agreement questionable in its enforceability, as understandings and recommendations are not legally binding like conditions and covenants.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Except as otherwise suggested above in this Amendment, all other terms, understandings and recommendations of the Agreement shall remain in full force and effect.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'specified', 'conditions', and 'covenants' to 'suggested', 'understandings', and 'recommendations' undermines the legally binding nature of the agreement. The contradicted text then references these weakened terms as if they maintain the original force. This creates an in-text contradiction because 'understandings' and 'recommendations' are not legally binding like 'conditions' and 'covenants', causing uncertainty about the enforceability of the agreement, as the contradicted section repeats the same weakened language making the whole section non-enforceable.
